question.title: Why is it recommended to call MultiDex.install() in attachBaseContext(Context)?
question.body:
I am learning multiDex in android platform. Here is https://developer.android.com/tools/building/multidex.html#mdex-gradle. When I use multiDex support library, the output apk will contain multi dex files such as class.dex, class1.dex, class2.dex. Then I write a class named myApplication inherited from Application and override the api attachBaseContext. In the method, I call MultiDex.install() just as recommended.

My question is that what would happen if I used MultiDex.install() in onCreate?

I know attachBaseContext is called before onCreate, so called in attachBaseContext is better.Howerver, actually I want to know is that why is it recommended just here? Is it a situation that ClassNotFoundException is threw when I call it in onCreate?
